test_cases.title: View day-off list for current year
test_cases.preconditions:
Logged-in employee with salary office that has public holidays.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM employee e JOIN office o ON e.office_id = o.id WHERE o.id IN (SELECT DISTINCT office_id FROM calendar) AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Vacations page (/vacation/my)
3. Click the 'Days off' tab
4. Verify the page displays a table with columns: Date of event, Duration, Reason, Approved by, Status, Actions
5. Verify the year selector shows the current year
6. Verify rows display public holidays for the employee's salary office
test_cases.expected_result: Day-off table shows all public holidays for the employee's salary office for the current year. Each row has date, duration (0 or 8), reason text, and status.
test_cases.notes: Duration: 0 = day-off (debit), 8 = worked holiday (credit), 7 = half-day

test_cases.title: Navigate to Days off tab from Vacations tab
test_cases.preconditions: Logged-in employee
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Vacations page (/vacation/my)
3. Verify 'Vacations' tab is active by default
4. Click 'Days off' tab
5. Verify URL changes to /vacation/my/daysoff
6. Verify day-off table is displayed
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation switches from Vacations to Days off tab without redirect to /sick-leave/my. URL is /vacation/my/daysoff.
test_cases.notes: Known navigation bug: Days off tab sometimes redirects to /sick-leave/my. Verify this does NOT happen.

test_cases.title: Create transfer request (reschedule day-off to future date)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a future public holiday that has no existing transfer request and duration=0.
Query: SELECT e.login, ed.public_date FROM employee e JOIN employee_dayoff ed ON ed.employee_id = e.id WHERE ed.duration = 0 AND ed.public_date > CURRENT_DATE AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM employee_dayoff_request edr WHERE edr.employee_id = e.id AND edr.public_date = ed.public_date) AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
3. Find the target public holiday row (duration=0, no existing transfer)
4. Click the edit (pencil) icon on that row
5. Verify 'Reschedule an event' modal opens
6. Verify 'Day off date' field shows the original date (read-only)
7. Select a valid future working day in the calendar picker
8. Verify OK button becomes enabled after date selection
9. Click OK
10. Verify success notification appears
11. Verify the row now shows arrow format: 'originalDate -> personalDate' with NEW status
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/{id} to remove the created request
test_cases.expected_result: Transfer request created successfully. Row displays 'originalDate -> personalDate' arrow format. Status is NEW. Edit and Cancel buttons appear on the row.
test_cases.notes: TransferDaysoffModal: minDate=originalDate for future dates. maxDate=end of next year.

test_cases.title: Edit transfer request (change personal date)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an existing NEW transfer request.
SETUP: Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff to create a transfer request if none exists.
Query: SELECT e.login, edr.id, edr.personal_date FROM employee e JOIN employee_dayoff_request edr ON edr.employee_id = e.id WHERE edr.status = 'NEW' AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a transfer request for the employee if none in NEW status exists
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
3. Find the NEW transfer request row (shows arrow format)
4. Click the edit (pencil) icon
5. Verify 'Reschedule an event' modal opens with current personal date shown
6. Select a different valid future working day
7. Click OK
8. Verify the row updates with the new personalDate in arrow format
9. Verify status remains NEW
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the transfer request
test_cases.expected_result: Personal date updated successfully. Arrow format shows new date. Status remains NEW. Optional approvers remain in ASKED status (not reset).
test_cases.notes: Only personalDate is changeable via PATCH. Optional approvers stay ASKED after edit.

test_cases.title: Cancel pending transfer request (NEW status)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a NEW transfer request.
SETUP: Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff to create one.
Query: SELECT e.login, edr.id FROM employee e JOIN employee_dayoff_request edr ON edr.employee_id = e.id WHERE edr.status = 'NEW' AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a transfer request for the employee
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
3. Find the NEW transfer request row
4. Click the red X (cancel) button on the row
5. Verify confirmation dialog appears (if any)
6. Confirm cancellation
7. Verify the transfer request row disappears or status changes to DELETED
8. Verify the original holiday row reverts to its base state (no arrow format)
test_cases.expected_result: Transfer request is deleted. Row reverts to original day-off display without arrow format.
test_cases.notes: UI uses DELETE endpoint, not PUT /cancel. Cancel X only visible on NEW status rows.

test_cases.title: Year selector changes displayed holidays
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with calendars spanning multiple years.
Query: SELECT DISTINCT e.login FROM employee e JOIN calendar c ON c.office_id = e.office_id WHERE c.year IN (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE), EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) - 1) AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
3. Note the number of holidays displayed for current year
4. Click the year selector datepicker
5. Select the previous year
6. Verify the table reloads with holidays for the selected year
7. Verify holiday count may differ between years
8. Select current year again and verify original data returns
test_cases.expected_result: Year selector correctly filters holidays by year. Different years may show different holiday counts based on calendar configuration.
test_cases.notes: BUG-DO-32: No calendar events before 2024 reported. Verify this is fixed.

test_cases.title: Verify holidays per salary office — Russia
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in Russia salary office.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM employee e JOIN office o ON e.office_id = o.id WHERE o.country_code = 'RU' AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the Russian-office employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
3. Count the number of public holidays displayed
4. Verify Russia has approximately 14-17 public holidays per year
5. Verify typical Russian holidays are present (e.g., New Year 1-8 Jan, Feb 23, Mar 8, May 1, May 9, Jun 12, Nov 4)
DB-CHECK: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM calendar_day cd JOIN calendar c ON cd.calendar_id = c.id JOIN office o ON c.office_id = o.id WHERE o.country_code = 'RU' AND c.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) AND cd.duration = 0
test_cases.expected_result: Russian office shows 14-17 public holidays matching the official Russian production calendar.
test_cases.notes: Russia typically has the most holidays. Exact count depends on year and working-weekend transfers.

test_cases.title: Verify holidays per salary office — Cyprus
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in Cyprus salary office.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM employee e JOIN office o ON e.office_id = o.id WHERE o.country_code = 'CY' AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the Cyprus-office employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
3. Count the number of public holidays displayed
4. Verify Cyprus has approximately 6-8 public holidays per year
5. Compare count with Russia office — should be fewer
test_cases.expected_result: Cyprus office shows fewer holidays than Russia (approximately 6-8 vs 14-17).

test_cases.title: NEW status displays arrow format (originalDate -> personalDate)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a NEW transfer request.
SETUP: Via API — create transfer request if needed.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff to create a transfer request
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
3. Find the NEW transfer request row
4. Verify the Date column shows arrow format: 'DD.MM.YYYY (day) -> DD.MM.YYYY (day)'
5. Verify the first date is the original public holiday date (lastApprovedDate)
6. Verify the second date is the selected personal date
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the transfer request

test_cases.notes: Date format: DD.MM.YYYY (abbreviated weekday), e.g., '01.05.2026 (fr)'

test_cases.title: APPROVED status displays only lastApprovedDate

Employee with an APPROVED transfer request.

3. Find the APPROVED transfer request row
4. Verify the Date column shows only the lastApprovedDate (single date, no arrow)
5. Verify status column shows 'Approved'
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the request
test_cases.expected_result: APPROVED rows show only the last approved date, not the arrow format. Status is 'Approved'.

test_cases.title: Edit button visibility — only future dates with duration=0
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with both future (duration=0) and past public holidays.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM employee e JOIN employee_dayoff ed ON ed.employee_id = e.id WHERE ed.duration = 0 AND ed.public_date > CURRENT_DATE AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
3. Find a future holiday row with duration=0
4. Verify edit (pencil) icon IS present
5. Find a past holiday row
6. Verify edit icon is NOT present on past rows
7. Find a row with duration=8 (worked holiday/credit)
8. Verify edit icon is NOT present on duration=8 rows
test_cases.expected_result: Edit button appears only on future rows with duration=0. Past dates and duration=8 (credit) rows have no edit button.
test_cases.notes: Edit button logic: NOT weekend AND duration=0 AND lastApprovedDate >= today

test_cases.title: Cancel button only on NEW status rows
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with requests in various statuses (NEW, APPROVED).
SETUP: Via API — create one NEW request and one APPROVED request.
test_cases.steps:

1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
3. Find the NEW status transfer row
4. Verify red X (cancel) button IS present
5. Find the APPROVED status transfer row
6. Verify red X (cancel) button is NOT present
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE both requests
test_cases.expected_result: Cancel X button appears only on NEW status rows. APPROVED and other statuses do not show cancel button.

test_cases.title: Transfer modal date constraints — min boundary (future original date)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a future public holiday (duration=0, no existing request).
Query: SELECT e.login, ed.public_date FROM employee e JOIN employee_dayoff ed ON ed.employee_id = e.id WHERE ed.duration = 0 AND ed.public_date > CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '30 days' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM employee_dayoff_request edr WHERE edr.employee_id = e.id AND edr.public_date = ed.public_date) AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
3. Click edit on a future holiday row
4. In the calendar picker, verify dates BEFORE the original date are disabled/greyed out
5. Verify the original date itself is selectable as minimum
6. Try to click a date before the original — verify it cannot be selected
7. Click Cancel to close modal
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar picker disables all dates before the original public holiday date. Minimum selectable date equals the original date.
test_cases.notes: Frontend: minDate = moment(originalDate) when original date is in the future

test_cases.title: Transfer modal date constraints — max boundary (end of next year)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a public holiday in January of current year.
Query: SELECT e.login, ed.public_date FROM employee e JOIN employee_dayoff ed ON ed.employee_id = e.id WHERE ed.duration = 0 AND EXTRACT(MONTH FROM ed.public_date) <= 3 AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM ed.public_date) = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
3. Click edit on an early-year holiday row
4. In the calendar picker, navigate forward to December of NEXT year
5. Verify Dec 31 of next year is the last selectable date
6. Try to navigate to January of the year after next — verify it's disabled
7. Click Cancel
test_cases.expected_result: Maximum transfer date is December 31 of the next year relative to the original date.
test_cases.notes: Frontend: maxDate = moment(originalDate).add(1, 'year').endOf('year')

test_cases.title: Transfer calendar disables existing day-off dates
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with multiple day-offs including at least one existing transfer request.
SETUP: Via API — create a transfer request to ensure a personalDate exists.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a transfer for employee to occupy a personalDate
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
3. Click edit on a different holiday (not the one with existing transfer)
4. In the calendar picker, find the date occupied by the existing transfer's personalDate
5. Verify that date is disabled/greyed in the calendar
6. Click Cancel
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the setup transfer request
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar picker disables dates already used by other transfer requests (personalDates).
test_cases.notes: Prevents duplicate personalDate assignment via UI

test_cases.title: Working weekend dates selectable in transfer calendar
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in Russia office (has working weekends in calendar).
Query: SELECT e.login, cd.date FROM employee e JOIN office o ON e.office_id = o.id JOIN calendar c ON c.office_id = o.id JOIN calendar_day cd ON cd.calendar_id = c.id WHERE o.country_code = 'RU' AND cd.duration = 8 AND EXTRACT(DOW FROM cd.date) IN (0, 6) AND cd.date > CURRENT_DATE AND c.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the Russian-office employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
3. Click edit on a future holiday
4. In the calendar picker, find a Saturday or Sunday that is a designated working weekend
5. Verify that date IS selectable (not greyed out despite being Sat/Sun)
6. Verify regular weekends (non-working) remain disabled
7. Click Cancel
test_cases.expected_result: Working weekends (Sat/Sun marked as working in production calendar) are selectable. Regular weekends remain disabled.
test_cases.notes: Working weekend exception: calendar entries marking Sat/Sun as working re-enable those dates

test_cases.title: OK button disabled until date selected in transfer modal

Manager with a pending NEW day-off transfer request from a subordinate.

test_cases.title: Navigate all 5 manager sub-tabs
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager user.

test_cases.steps:

test_cases.title: Path A — Calendar day moved, transfer request follows

CLEANUP: Revert calendar change; DELETE transfer request

test_cases.notes: BUG-DO-8: Orphaned ledger entry on calendar conflict move. Path A is silent — no notification.

SETUP: Via API — create and approve a transfer request

DB-CHECK: Verify vacation recalculation triggered
CLEANUP: Restore calendar day if needed
test_cases.expected_result: Request status changes to DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR. Ledger entries physically deleted. Vacation balance recalculated.
test_cases.notes: Path B: physically deletes ledger + recalculates vacation. Most destructive cascade.

test_cases.expected_result: System automatically rejects NEW requests in the affected period. Requests in other periods remain unchanged.

DB-CHECK: Verify status per employee matches expected isolation
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar deletion only affects employees in the modified office. Other offices with same-date holidays are not impacted.
test_cases.notes: BUG-DO-21: Cross-calendar deletion bug where same-date holidays in different offices interfered

test_cases.title: Path A orphaned ledger — explicit verification (BUG-DO-8)
test_cases.preconditions:
Admin access. Employee with an APPROVED transfer request.

SETUP: Via API — create and approve transfer request

SETUP: Via API — create and approve transfer.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create and approve transfer request
1. Note employee's vacation balance before
2. Trigger auto-deletion (calendar day removal)
3. Verify request status = DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR
DB-CHECK: Verify vacation balance is recalculated (not permanently lost)
4. Compare balance: should be restored to pre-transfer level
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation balance must be updated (recalculated) after auto-deletion. No permanent vacation day loss.
test_cases.notes: Two fix attempts needed. MR !4592. Permanent vacation day loss reported.

test_cases.title: Regression: Vacation not recalculated on transfer to vacation date (BUG-DO-23, #2833)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with an approved vacation. Day-off transfer targeting a date within the vacation period.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create vacation, approve it. Then create day-off transfer to a date within vacation period.
1. Verify the system handles the conflict between day-off transfer and existing vacation
2. Verify vacation is recalculated if the transfer overlaps
DB-CHECK: Verify vacation days adjusted
test_cases.expected_result: When a day-off is transferred onto a date within an existing vacation, the vacation should be recalculated.
test_cases.notes: Production bug — real users affected. Cross-feature: day-off × vacation interaction.

test_cases.title: Regression: Access Denied silent failure on transfer (BUG-DO-24, #2962)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee attempting a transfer that should fail due to permissions.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a scenario where the employee lacks permission for the transfer
1. Login as the employee
2. Attempt to create a transfer request
3. If the API returns 400/403, verify the UI displays a visible error notification
4. Verify the error is NOT silent (no invisible 400 with no UI feedback)
test_cases.expected_result: Permission failures should show a visible error banner/notification to the user, not fail silently.
test_cases.notes: BUG-DO-24: 400 response but no error banner shown to user

test_cases.title: Regression: 500 on reused freed date (BUG-DO-25, #2801)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee who creates transfer A→B, deletes it (freeing date B), then tries to reuse date B.
SETUP: Via API — create transfer to date B, then delete it.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create transfer (personalDate=B), then DELETE it
1. Via API — create a new transfer with personalDate=B (the freed date)
2. Verify the request succeeds (200/201) without 500 error
3. If 500: verify it's the DB unique constraint violation
test_cases.expected_result: Reusing a freed personalDate should succeed. Previously caused 500 DB constraint violation (BUG-DO-25).
test_cases.notes: Unique constraint violation when editing transfer to reuse a freed date

test_cases.title: Regression: Re-transfer blocked after 7h shortened day (BUG-DO-27, #2874)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a transfer to a 7h (shortened) day.
SETUP: Via API — create transfer where personalDate falls on a shortened day.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create transfer to a 7h day, then attempt to re-transfer
1. Via API — PATCH the request with a new personalDate
2. Verify the re-transfer succeeds
3. If blocked: confirm BUG-DO-27
test_cases.expected_result: Re-transferring a day-off that was on a 7h shortened day should be allowed.
test_cases.notes: Feature + 3 bugs during backward re-transfer implementation

test_cases.title: Regression: Pending transfer shown with wrong calendar colors (BUG-DO-29, #3094)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with a pending (NEW) transfer request.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a NEW transfer request
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to the availability chart / calendar view
3. Find the date of the pending transfer
4. Verify the calendar color coding is correct (pending transfer color, not confirmed)
5. Compare with approved transfer colors
test_cases.expected_result: Pending transfers should show distinct color from approved transfers in the calendar view.
test_cases.notes: Calendar display bug — pending transfer shown with wrong colors

test_cases.title: Regression: Availability chart events missing (BUG-DO-31, #3312)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with day-off transfers visible in the system.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to the availability chart
3. Verify day-off events are visible in the chart
4. Verify events appear for the correct date ranges
5. Zoom out to quarterly view — verify events still visible
test_cases.expected_result: All day-off events should appear in the availability chart. No missing events.
test_cases.notes: Events missing for unfiltered query. HIGH severity.

test_cases.title: Regression: No calendar events before 2024 (BUG-DO-32, #3292)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with historical data before 2024.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
3. Select year 2023 in the year picker
4. Verify whether day-off records for 2023 appear
5. If empty: confirm BUG-DO-32 (pre-2024 data missing)
test_cases.expected_result: Day-off records from 2023 and earlier should be visible when the corresponding year is selected.
test_cases.notes: Reported: no calendar events before 2024 in certain views

test_cases.title: Regression: Calendar not set after reinstatement with SO change (BUG-DO-33, #3212)
test_cases.preconditions: Admin access. Employee who was terminated and reinstated to a different salary office.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via admin — disable employee (simulate termination)
2. Via admin — re-enable employee with a different salary office
3. Login as the reinstated employee
4. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
5. Verify the employee sees the NEW office's production calendar holidays
DB-CHECK: Verify calendar entries exist for the employee's new office
test_cases.expected_result: Reinstated employee with new salary office should see the correct production calendar for their new office.
test_cases.notes: Gap in reinstatement flow when salary office changes

test_cases.title: Regression: Auto-delete event not generated (BUG-DO-34, #2736)
test_cases.preconditions: Scenario where auto-deletion should generate a domain event.
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger auto-deletion of day-off transfers (via calendar change)
2. Verify the DayOffAutoDeletedEvent is fired
3. Verify downstream listeners (vacation recalculation, norm update) are triggered
DB-CHECK: Verify vacation_days recalculated after auto-delete event
test_cases.expected_result: Auto-deletion must fire the appropriate domain event triggering vacation recalculation and norm updates.
test_cases.notes: Bug: event not generated, vacation left with 0 days. Part of event feed implementation (3 bugs).

test_cases.title: Regression: Accrued days not returned on AV=False conversion (BUG-DO-35, #2736)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in AV=False office with accrued vacation days.
Query: SELECT e.login, vd.days FROM employee e JOIN office o ON e.office_id = o.id WHERE o.advance_vacation = false AND vd.days > 0 AND e.enabled = true LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger day-off auto-deletion for AV=False employee
2. Verify the conversion to Administrative leave does NOT consume accrued days permanently
DB-CHECK: Verify vacation_days.days restored or correctly adjusted
test_cases.expected_result: Accrued vacation days must be returned to balance when auto-deletion triggers conversion in AV=False offices.
test_cases.notes: Accrued days not returned to balance. Part of event feed implementation bugs.

test_cases.title: Regression: Hardcoded date 2024-03-10 in WeekendTableActions (BUG-DO-13)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee viewing day-off table.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my/daysoff
3. Interact with the table action buttons
4. Verify no action button behavior is affected by the hardcoded date 2024-03-10
5. If current date is near 2024-03-10: verify isOnlyOneAction logic works correctly
test_cases.expected_result: Action button logic should not depend on a hardcoded date. Behavior should be consistent regardless of current date.
test_cases.notes: Hardcoded date in WeekendTableActions.isOnlyOneAction — may cause incorrect button visibility

test_cases.title: Regression: Confirmed transfer survives SO calendar change (BUG-DO-19, #2971)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with APPROVED transfer. Admin access to modify next year's SO calendar.
SETUP: Via API — create and approve a transfer for next year.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create and approve transfer request with publicDate in next year
1. Login as admin
2. Modify next year's salary office calendar (e.g., remove the public holiday)
3. Verify the APPROVED transfer request's status
4. Expected: should be set to DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR
5. Actual: may survive the change (BUG-DO-19 — OPEN bug, no design spec)
DB-CHECK: SELECT status FROM employee_dayoff_request WHERE id = <id>
test_cases.expected_result: Expected: APPROVED transfer should be affected by calendar change. Actual: BUG-DO-19 reports it survives. This is an OPEN bug with no design spec.
test_cases.notes: OPEN BUG. No design spec for what should happen. Confirmed transfer survives SO calendar change for next year.

test_cases.title: Regression: Navigation bug — Days off redirects to sick-leave
test_cases.preconditions: Logged-in employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my (Vacations tab)
3. Click 'Days off' tab
4. Verify URL is /vacation/my/daysoff (NOT /sick-leave/my)
5. Repeat navigation 3-5 times to check for intermittent redirect
6. Try navigating from different pages (reports, settings) to Days off tab
test_cases.expected_result: Days off tab always navigates to /vacation/my/daysoff. Never redirects to /sick-leave/my.
test_cases.notes: Intermittent navigation bug documented during UI exploration

test_cases.title: Regression: Hardcoded production URL in notifications (BUG-DO-10)
test_cases.preconditions: Access to notification email content.
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger a day-off notification (approval/rejection)
2. Inspect the email content
3. Verify all URLs in the email point to the correct environment (not hardcoded production URL)
DB-CHECK: Check notification template for environment-specific URL
test_cases.expected_result: Notification emails should use environment-specific URLs, not hardcoded production URLs.
test_cases.notes: BUG-DO-10: Hardcoded production URL in notification templates

test_cases.title: Regression: updateEmployeeDayoffRequest drops personalDate (BUG-DO-14)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a transfer request.
SETUP: Via API — create request with personalDate.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create transfer request with personalDate=X
1. Via API — update the request via the UI's update path (PUT/PATCH)
2. Verify personalDate is NOT silently dropped
DB-CHECK: SELECT personal_date FROM employee_dayoff_request WHERE id = <id> — verify still X
test_cases.expected_result: personalDate should be preserved after update operations. BUG-DO-14 reported silent drop via destructuring.
test_cases.notes: Frontend bug: updateEmployeeDayoffRequest drops personalDate silently via destructuring

test_cases.title: Regression: Transaction isolation — ledger write and status not atomic (BUG-DO-15)
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager-subordinate pair.
SETUP: Via API — create NEW transfer request.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create NEW transfer request
1. Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/employee-dayOff/{id}/approve
2. Immediately (within 1 second) — query request status AND ledger entries
DB-CHECK: SELECT status FROM employee_dayoff_request WHERE id = <id>
DB-CHECK: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employee_dayoff_ledger WHERE employee_dayoff_request_id = <id>
3. Verify BOTH status update AND ledger write completed (or both rolled back)
4. Check for inconsistent state: status=APPROVED but ledger empty, or status=NEW but ledger populated
test_cases.expected_result: Status update and ledger write should be atomic (transactional). If one fails, both should roll back. BUG-DO-15 reports non-atomicity risk.
test_cases.notes: BUG-DO-15: Ledger write and status update not wrapped in single transaction. Race condition potential.

test_cases.title: Regression: Day-off + sick leave individual norm interaction (BUG-DO-26, #2901)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with both a transferred day-off and a sick leave in the same month.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave and transfer day-off to same month.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for 3 days in month X. Create and approve day-off transfer with personalDate in month X.
1. Query the employee's individual norm for month X
DB-CHECK: SELECT hours FROM individual_norm WHERE employee_id = <id> AND month = X
2. Verify norm accounts for BOTH the sick leave deduction AND the day-off transfer
3. Verify norm calculation: standard_norm - sick_leave_days + day_off_credit (or equivalent)
4. Delete the day-off transfer
5. Verify norm is recalculated removing only the day-off component
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE sick leave and transfer request
test_cases.expected_result: Individual norm correctly accounts for both sick leave and day-off transfer in the same month. Each component is independently calculated and combined.
test_cases.notes: BUG-DO-26: Individual norm recalculation with sick leave + day-off interaction. Production bug.

test_cases.title: Regression: Auto-deleted vacation converted to Administrative with 0 days (#3223 second fix)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in AV=False office with Regular vacation and approved day-off transfer.
SETUP: Create vacation and day-off transfer, trigger auto-deletion.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create Regular vacation for AV=False employee, create and approve day-off transfer
1. Trigger auto-deletion by removing the calendar day
2. Verify day-off request status = DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR
DB-CHECK: SELECT type, days FROM vacation WHERE employee_id = <id> AND type = 'ADMINISTRATIVE'
3. Verify NO Administrative vacation with 0 days was created
4. Verify the Regular vacation was properly handled (deleted or days restored, not converted to Administrative with 0)
CLEANUP: Restore calendar if needed
test_cases.expected_result: Auto-deletion should NOT create Administrative vacations with 0 days. This was a regression in the second fix attempt for #3223.
test_cases.notes: Second regression from #3223: auto-deleted Regular vacations converted to Administrative with 0 days instead of being properly deleted.

test_cases.expected_result: Date navigation updates displayed date and assignment data. Total row reflects correct sums for selected date.

test_cases.title: Search for task by name

test_cases.title: Notification banners display correctly

test_cases.title: Add task via search bar — happy path

Employee assigned to at least one project with tasks.

2. Navigate to Tasks tab

8. Verify Total row updates

test_cases.title: Edit hours in effort cell — inline editing

Employee with an existing assignment for today.

test_cases.title: Edit comment in comment cell

test_cases.expected_result: DnD handles are conditional on editing mode. Not visible in read-only state.

test_cases.title: DnD reorder persists after page reload
test_cases.preconditions: Tasks reordered via DnD.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM, open editing, reorder tasks (move C above A)
2. Refresh the page (F5)
3. Re-navigate to the same project and employee
4. Verify the custom order is preserved
DB-CHECK: Verify next_assignment chain reflects DnD order
test_cases.expected_result: DnD order persists in DB via next_assignment linked-list pointers. Not just frontend state.

test_cases.title: Bug #3332 — DnD should not create duplicate task rows
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with 3+ assignments. Editing mode.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM, open editing
2. Drag a task to a new position
3. Carefully observe: does a duplicate row appear?
4. Count rows before and after DnD — should be the same
5. Repeat DnD on the same task 3 times
6. Verify no ghost/duplicate entries
test_cases.expected_result: No duplicate rows after DnD. Bug #3332 root cause: generateTaskAssignments.ts appends new ID without removing old from order array. Verify this is fixed.
test_cases.notes: Root cause identified: splice commented out with TODO. Regression test.

test_cases.title: Bug #3314 — task order preserved after 'Open for Editing'
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with DnD-ordered tasks.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM, open editing on employee A
2. Reorder tasks for employee A via DnD
3. Close editing for employee A
4. Open editing for employee B (different employee)
5. Go back to employee A — verify their task order is PRESERVED
6. Click 'Open for editing' on employee A again
7. Verify order is still the DnD custom order (not reset)
test_cases.expected_result: DnD order not disrupted by toggling 'Open for editing'. Bug #3314: TasksPlannerTable.tsx re-sorts on readOnly state change. Verify fix.
test_cases.notes: Root cause: useEffect with .sort() triggers on every rowsMap change, disrupting DnD order.

test_cases.title: Task ordering rules — non-generated above generated
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with mixed assignment types.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to Tasks tab or Projects tab
2. Verify non-generated assignments appear ABOVE generated ones
3. Open for editing (generates assignments)
4. Verify order preserved: existing tasks remain in their positions
test_cases.expected_result: Ordering rule: non-generated (real) above generated. Order preserved when opening for editing.

test_cases.title: Lock acquired when editing starts
test_cases.preconditions:
Two users with access to the same project. Both logged in.
Note: Requires two browser sessions.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as User A, navigate to Projects tab, open editing
2. Click on hours cell for Employee X's task → cell becomes editable
3. In a second browser, login as User B, navigate to same project
4. Verify User B sees the cell as locked (shows User A's name or lock indicator)
5. Verify User B cannot edit that cell while locked
test_cases.expected_result: Lock acquired on cell focus. REST POST /v1/assignments/lock publishes LOCK event via WebSocket. Other users see lock in real-time.
test_cases.notes: Requires multi-user testing with two browser sessions. Backend uses Caffeine cache (in-memory, no DB).

test_cases.title: Auto-release lock after 1 minute TTL
test_cases.preconditions: User A has locked a cell.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A clicks on a cell (acquires lock)
2. User A does NOT interact further (leaves cell focused but idle)
3. Wait 60+ seconds
4. In User B's browser, verify the cell becomes editable (lock released)
5. Verify UNLOCK WebSocket event received by User B
test_cases.expected_result: Lock auto-releases after 1 minute TTL. Caffeine cache TTL expiry triggers release. WebSocket UNLOCK event published.
test_cases.notes: In-memory Caffeine cache — locks lost on server restart.

test_cases.title: Manual unlock on blur/navigation
test_cases.preconditions: User has locked a cell by focusing on it.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A clicks on hours cell (lock acquired)
2. User A clicks on a different cell or navigates away
3. Verify the original cell's lock is released immediately
4. Verify UNLOCK WebSocket event sent
5. Verify User B can now edit the original cell
test_cases.expected_result: Lock released on blur. REST DELETE /v1/assignments/unlock. Immediate release, not waiting for TTL.

test_cases.title: Lock types — effort, comment, remainingEstimate
test_cases.preconditions: Two users on same project.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A locks the effort cell → verify only effort locked, comment still editable by User B
2. User A releases effort lock
3. User A locks the comment cell → verify only comment locked
4. User A releases comment lock
5. User A locks remainingEstimate → verify only that field locked
test_cases.expected_result: Locks are per-field, not per-row. FieldsToLockType: effort, comment, remainingEstimate, ALL_ROW.

test_cases.title: Cell selections visible to other users (focus tracking)
test_cases.preconditions: Two users on same project.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A selects a cell (without editing)
2. Verify User B sees User A's selection highlighted (colored indicator)
3. User A moves to a different cell
4. Verify User B sees selection move in real-time
5. Verify selection colors distinguish between users
test_cases.expected_result: Cell selections broadcast via WebSocket SELECT event. MappedSelection includes ownerLogin, color, group, date.
test_cases.notes: Focus tracking is separate from lock mechanism. Selections are visual-only, not blocking.

test_cases.title: Refresh tickets — updates metadata only
test_cases.preconditions: Project with tracker integration configured. PM logged in.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Projects tab, select a tracker-integrated project
3. Open for editing
4. Click 'Refresh tickets' button on an employee's row
5. Verify loading state appears
6. Verify task names/ticket info update (metadata refresh)
7. Verify hours are NOT changed (refresh is metadata-only)
test_cases.expected_result: Refresh only updates task names, priorities, ticket_info. Does NOT import or modify work hours. fetchProjectTasksRefresh saga.
test_cases.notes: #3394 distinguishes: Refresh=metadata, Load=metadata+hours.

test_cases.title: Upload hours to tracker (TTT → tracker)
test_cases.preconditions: Project with tracker integration. Employee has reported hours. Tracker credentials configured.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Projects tab, open editing
3. Click 'Actions' dropdown
4. Click 'Upload to Tracker' (or equivalent)
5. Verify loading state during sync
6. Verify success notification: 'planner.tracker.success'
7. Verify no assignment data changed in TTT (one-way upload)
test_cases.expected_result: Hours uploaded to external tracker. Source: TTT. Uses handleSyncWorkLogInfoWithTracker saga. POST /tracker-work-log/sync with source='TTT'.
test_cases.notes: Requires employee tracker credentials configured at /v1/employees/current/settings/trackers.

test_cases.title: Download hours from tracker (tracker → TTT)
test_cases.preconditions: Project with tracker integration. Tracker has worklogs.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Click 'Actions' dropdown > 'Download hours from the tracker'
3. Verify loading state
4. Verify hours imported from tracker appear in planner
5. Verify success notification: 'planner.tracker.hours_uploaded'
test_cases.expected_result: Hours downloaded from external tracker. Source: TRACKER. Uses handleSyncProjectWithTracker saga.

test_cases.title: Tracker sync error — no tracker configured
test_cases.preconditions: Project WITHOUT tracker integration or without credentials.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM of a project without tracker configuration
2. Click 'Refresh tickets' or 'Update tickets' on an employee
3. Verify error banner: 'Failed to update tickets for the project X. Project manager has to set up synchronization with the tracker'
4. Verify red error banner is displayed
test_cases.expected_result: Error message displayed when tracker not configured. exception.tracker.not.supported or exception.no.tracker.url.found.
test_cases.notes: Banner text observed on qa-1 session 74.

test_cases.title: WebSocket TRACKER_SYNC events
test_cases.preconditions: Two users viewing same project. Tracker sync triggered.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A triggers tracker sync (Upload or Download)
2. Verify User B receives TRACKER_SYNC_START event (loading indicator?)
3. Wait for sync to complete
4. Verify User B receives TRACKER_SYNC_FINISH event
5. Verify User B's planner view updates after sync
test_cases.expected_result: WebSocket events on /topic/projects/{projectId}/tracker-work-log. TRACKER_SYNC_START and TRACKER_SYNC_FINISH broadcast to all viewers.
test_cases.notes: Project-level topic — all project viewers see sync status.

test_cases.title: 'Actions' dropdown — 'Approve all'
test_cases.preconditions: PM with editing mode active. Employees have reported hours.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Projects tab, open editing
3. Click 'Actions' dropdown
4. Click 'Approve all'
5. Verify all reported hours for the project are approved
6. Verify approval status changes in the table
test_cases.expected_result: 'Approve all' approves all employee reports for the project on the selected date.

test_cases.title: Open Project Settings modal
test_cases.preconditions: PM of a project.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Projects tab, select project
3. Click the Project Settings icon (gear at far right of header)
4. Verify 'Project settings' modal opens
5. Verify two tabs: 'Project members' and 'Tasks closing'
6. Verify 'Project members' tab is active by default
7. Verify employee list is visible
test_cases.expected_result: Modal opens with two tabs. Default: Project members. Dialog role='dialog' with heading 'Project settings'.
test_cases.notes: Settings icon is hard to find — unnamed SVG inside .planner__project-group-add.

test_cases.title: Add member to project
test_cases.preconditions:
PM of a project. At least one employee not already a member.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e WHERE e.enabled = true AND e.id NOT IN (SELECT employee_id FROM ttt_backend.project_member WHERE project_id = {projectId}) ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Open Project Settings > Project members tab
2. Click the employee dropdown (combobox)
3. Type an employee name
4. Select the employee from dropdown
5. Click the '+' add button
6. Verify 'Changes saved' notification appears
7. Verify new member appears in the members table
8. Verify the added employee no longer appears in the dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Member added immediately. 'Changes saved' notification. Added employee filtered from dropdown.

test_cases.title: Remove member from project
test_cases.preconditions: Project with at least 2 members. PM logged in.
test_cases.steps:
1. Open Project Settings > Project members
2. Find a member in the table
3. Click the delete (trash) icon on their row
4. Verify 'Changes saved' notification
5. Verify member removed from the table
6. Verify the removed employee reappears in the dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Member removed immediately. Notification displayed. Employee available in dropdown again.

test_cases.title: Add/edit project role for member
test_cases.preconditions: PM with project member in the list.
test_cases.steps:
1. Open Project Settings > Project members
2. Find a member without a role (or with an existing role)
3. Click on the role field (placeholder: 'Click to add role' or existing role text)
4. Type a role (e.g., 'Developer')
5. Press Enter or click away to save
6. Verify 'Changes saved' notification
7. Verify role displayed next to member name
test_cases.expected_result: Inline role editing. Click to edit, Enter to save. Role stored in project_member table.
test_cases.notes: Placeholder: 'Add a role' per Confluence §7.3.2.

test_cases.title: Member order determines planner display order
test_cases.preconditions: Project with 3+ members. Members reordered via DnD in settings.
test_cases.steps:
1. Open Project Settings > Project members
2. Reorder members via DnD (if supported) or note current order
3. Click OK to close
4. In the Projects tab, verify employee group rows appear in the same order as the members list
5. Reorder in settings → verify planner order changes
test_cases.expected_result: Employee ordering in planner matches Project Settings > Project Members order. Per Confluence §6.1, #3375.
test_cases.notes: #3375 was a regression where employee ordering broke.

test_cases.title: Employee name links to CS profile
test_cases.preconditions: Project Settings open with members.
test_cases.steps:
1. Open Project Settings > Project members
2. Find a member with a linked name
3. Verify name is a clickable link
4. Click the link → verify it opens Company Staff profile (https://cs.noveogroup.com/profile/{id})
5. Verify link opens in new tab
test_cases.expected_result: Employee names link to Company Staff profiles. External link to CS system.

test_cases.title: Report period indicator — open vs closed
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with both open and closed report periods.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as employee
2. Navigate to Tasks tab
3. Navigate to a date in the current open period → verify editing is enabled
4. Navigate to a date in a past closed period → verify editing is DISABLED
5. Verify read-only indicator on closed-period assignments
test_cases.expected_result: Open period: editable cells. Closed period: read-only. EmployeeReportPeriod with status field controls editability.
test_cases.notes: Report periods managed via GET /v1/report-periods. isOpenPeriod determines editability.

test_cases.title: Cannot edit hours in closed report period
test_cases.preconditions: Date in a closed report period for the employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to a date in the employee's closed report period
2. Attempt to click on hours cell
3. Verify cell is NOT editable (no input appears)
4. Verify visual indication that period is closed (greyed out or read-only styling)
test_cases.expected_result: Hours cells are read-only in closed periods. 'Open for editing' button not available for closed periods.
test_cases.notes: isOpenPeriod === true required for editing.

test_cases.title: Total hours calculation per employee
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with multiple assignments with hours.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to Tasks tab
2. Verify Total row at bottom sums all project hours for the selected date
3. Edit hours in one task (e.g., change from 2 to 5)
4. Verify Total row updates (increases by 3)
5. Navigate to Projects tab, select a project
6. Verify per-employee totals in the employee header rows
test_cases.expected_result: Total row shows sum of all hours. Updates dynamically on edit. Both Tasks and Projects tabs show totals.
test_cases.notes: selectTotalEffortsByProject selector in plannerTasks slice.

test_cases.title: Assignment history — paginated view
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment with history of changes.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to planner and find an assignment
2. Open assignment history (if UI control exists)
3. Verify history shows: date, effort, comment, remainingEstimate changes
4. Verify pagination controls if many history entries
5. Verify sort options: date, effort, comment, remainingEstimate
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment history shows change log. Paginated. Sortable. TaskAssignmentHistoryItemDTO content.
test_cases.notes: sortDirection + sortField available. totalHours shown.

test_cases.title: #2914 — 36h daily limit warning displayed
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee who has reported >36h across all projects on a day.
SETUP: Via API — create task reports totaling >36h on one date.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create multiple task_reports for the employee on the same date, totaling >36h
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to planner / employee dashboard
3. Verify warning indicator appears for the date
4. Check employee warnings: GET /employee/current/warnings
5. Verify warning type: DATE_EFFORT_OVER_LIMIT
test_cases.expected_result: 36h daily limit triggers warning. TaskReportWarningCommand.findDaysWithExceededLimit() detects it. Warning is DISPLAY ONLY — no hard block.
test_cases.notes: ttt.daily-report-limit: 36h (2160 minutes). Warning only, not a hard block.

test_cases.title: #2914 — no hard block on exceeding 36h
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with 35h already reported on a date.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create task_reports totaling 35h on a date
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to planner Tasks tab
3. Edit hours on an additional task: enter '5' (bringing total to 40h)
4. Verify the edit is ACCEPTED (no blocking, no error)
5. Verify total shows 40h
6. Verify warning appears but does not prevent saving
test_cases.expected_result: No hard block. >36h is allowed. Warning displayed but save succeeds. No frontend or backend validation blocks submission.
test_cases.notes: Bypass: split hours across projects. Per-project check: 20h < 36h each, but total 40h.

test_cases.title: Per-project warning vs global warning
test_cases.preconditions: Employee reporting hours across 2 projects.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — report 25h in Project A and 20h in Project B (same date)
1. Via API — GET /project/{projectA}/warnings → verify NO per-project warning (25h < 36h)
2. Via API — GET /project/{projectB}/warnings → verify NO per-project warning (20h < 36h)
3. Via API — GET /employee/current/warnings → verify GLOBAL warning (45h > 36h)
4. Verify UI shows global warning but no per-project indicator
test_cases.expected_result: Per-project validation misses cross-project totals. Only global employee-wide check catches it. Design issue documented in #2914.
test_cases.notes: Root cause: per-project check uses task_report WHERE project = :id, not ALL projects.

test_cases.title: Read-only assignments cannot be edited
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with readOnly assignments (not opened for editing).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM, navigate to Projects tab
2. Find an employee NOT opened for editing
3. Verify their assignment cells are not editable
4. Verify action buttons in comment column are disabled/greyed
5. Verify no DnD handles visible
test_cases.expected_result: Read-only assignments: cells not editable, buttons disabled, no DnD handles. planner__cel--color-read-only CSS class.

test_cases.title: Real-time hours update via WebSocket (PATCH event)
test_cases.preconditions: Two users viewing the same project.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A and User B both navigate to same project in Projects tab
2. User A edits hours for Employee X's task (changes 2 → 5)
3. User A saves
4. Verify User B's view updates in real-time (shows 5 without refresh)
5. Verify Total row for User B also updates
test_cases.expected_result: WebSocket PATCH event on /topic/employees/{login}/assignments. Both plannerTasks and plannerProjects slices updated via manager saga routing.
test_cases.notes: Requires two browser sessions. Events dispatched to BOTH view slices.

test_cases.title: Real-time task addition via WebSocket (ADD event)
test_cases.preconditions: Two users on same project.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A adds a new task via search bar
2. Verify User B sees the new task appear in real-time
3. Verify User B's view includes the new task in the correct position (top of group)
4. Verify green highlight visible to User B
test_cases.expected_result: WebSocket ADD event broadcasts new assignment. Both users see it in real-time.

test_cases.title: Real-time task deletion via WebSocket (DELETE event)
test_cases.preconditions: Two users on same project.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A deletes an assignment
2. Verify User B's view removes the row in real-time
3. Verify User B's total recalculates
test_cases.expected_result: WebSocket DELETE event removes assignment from both users' views. Total recalculates.

test_cases.title: Task rename via WebSocket (TASK_RENAME event)
test_cases.preconditions: Two users viewing same project. Task rename triggered.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A opens task rename modal
2. User A changes task name from 'Old Name' to 'New Name'
3. User A saves
4. Verify User B sees 'New Name' in their planner view in real-time
test_cases.expected_result: WebSocket TASK_RENAME event updates task name for all viewers. No page refresh needed.
test_cases.notes: Task rename modal: modalTaskRename, taskRenameData in tasks slice.

test_cases.title: Generate event broadcasts to all users
test_cases.preconditions: Two users on same project. PM opens editing.
test_cases.steps:
1. User A (PM) clicks 'Open for editing' on an employee
2. Verify User B receives GENERATE WebSocket event
3. Verify User B sees the newly generated assignments appear
4. Verify debouncing prevents excessive re-renders
test_cases.expected_result: GENERATE event from /v1/assignments/generate broadcasts via WebSocket. Both users see generated assignments. Frontend debounces GENERATE events.

test_cases.title: Copy table — happy path copies visible data to clipboard
test_cases.preconditions:
PM logged in. Project with ≥2 employees and reported hours in current week.
Query: SELECT DISTINCT pa.project_id FROM planner_assignment pa JOIN project_employee pe ON pa.project_id = pe.project_id WHERE pe.role IN ('PM','LEAD') GROUP BY pa.project_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT pa.employee_id) >= 2 LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select project with reported hours
4. Click 'Open for editing' on an employee to enter editing mode
5. Click 'Copy table' button in the Actions dropdown
6. Open a text editor and paste clipboard content (Ctrl+V)
7. Verify pasted data contains employee names, task names, and hours for visible dates
test_cases.expected_result: Clipboard contains tab-separated table data matching the planner grid. Includes employee names, task rows, daily hours, and totals.
test_cases.notes: Copy table button only visible in editing mode. Uses navigator.clipboard.writeText API.

test_cases.title: Copy table — #3386 regression: deleted/closed tasks excluded
test_cases.preconditions:
PM logged in. Project where at least one task has been deleted or project has closed tasks.
Query: SELECT DISTINCT pa.project_id FROM planner_assignment pa JOIN task t ON pa.task_id = t.id WHERE t.deleted = true OR t.project_id IN (SELECT id FROM project WHERE status = 'CLOSED') LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select project known to have deleted/closed tasks
4. Enter editing mode
5. Click 'Copy table' from Actions dropdown
6. Paste clipboard into text editor
7. Verify no deleted or closed tasks appear in pasted data
test_cases.expected_result: Copied table excludes deleted tasks and tasks from closed projects. Only active, visible tasks are in clipboard data. Bug #3386 required filtering these out.
test_cases.notes: Regression test for #3386. Bug was that deleted/closed tasks were included in copy output.

test_cases.title: Copy table — button only available in editing mode
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with employees.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select a project
4. Verify 'Actions' dropdown does NOT contain 'Copy table' in view-only mode
5. Click 'Open for editing' on an employee
6. Open Actions dropdown
7. Verify 'Copy table' option is now visible
test_cases.expected_result: Copy table is unavailable in read-only mode. It appears only after entering editing mode via 'Open for editing'.
test_cases.notes: Actions dropdown contents change based on editing mode state.

test_cases.title: Copy table — empty project produces empty or no-data clipboard
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with no assignments for current week.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select a project with no employees or no assignments in current date range
4. Enter editing mode
5. Click 'Copy table' from Actions dropdown
6. Paste clipboard into text editor
7. Verify output is empty or contains only headers
test_cases.expected_result: Copy table handles empty state gracefully — clipboard contains header row only or is empty. No error occurs.

test_cases.title: Copy table — includes all employees and weekly totals
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with 3+ employees, each with hours in current week.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select project with 3+ employees having hours
4. Enter editing mode
5. Click 'Copy table'
6. Paste into spreadsheet application
7. Count employee rows vs visible employees in planner
8. Verify totals column matches planner weekly total
test_cases.expected_result: All visible employees are included. Weekly/daily totals match those displayed in the planner grid. Data is tab-separated and imports cleanly into spreadsheets.
test_cases.notes: Verify row count matches planner. Totals row should be at bottom.

test_cases.title: #3294 — Delete task on current date reflects immediately
test_cases.preconditions:
PM logged in. Project with at least one task that has assignments today.
Query: SELECT pa.project_id, pa.task_id FROM planner_assignment pa WHERE pa.date = CURRENT_DATE AND pa.hours > 0 LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select project and enter editing mode
4. Note the task name and current total hours
5. Right-click or use context menu on the task to delete
6. Confirm deletion in dialog
7. Verify task row disappears from the planner grid immediately
8. Verify total hours recalculates without the deleted task's hours
test_cases.expected_result: Task deletion on current date is reflected immediately in the UI. No manual refresh required. Total hours update to exclude deleted task.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3294: after deleting a task, the planner did not refresh and stale data remained visible.

test_cases.title: #3296 — Approved status preserved after tracker import
test_cases.preconditions:
PM logged in. Project with tracker configured. Employee with APPROVED status for current period.
Setup: Ensure at least one employee has approved hours for today.
Query: SELECT pa.employee_id, pa.project_id FROM planner_assignment pa WHERE pa.approved = true AND pa.date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days' LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab, select project with tracker
3. Note which employees have APPROVED status (green checkmark or indicator)
4. Open Actions dropdown → click 'Download hours from tracker'
5. Wait for sync to complete
6. Verify previously APPROVED employees still show APPROVED status
7. DB-CHECK: SELECT approved FROM planner_assignment WHERE employee_id = <id> AND date = <date>
test_cases.expected_result: Tracker import does NOT clear the APPROVED status on employees whose hours were already approved. Only unapproved hours are affected by import.
test_cases.requirement_ref: GitLab #3296 — approved status cleared after import
test_cases.notes: Bug #3296: tracker sync was resetting approved=false on all assignments regardless of prior approval state.

test_cases.title: #3375 — Employee order matches Project Members DnD order
test_cases.preconditions:
PM logged in. Project with 3+ employees.
Query: SELECT pe.project_id FROM project_employee pe GROUP BY pe.project_id HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3 ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab, select project
3. Note the order of employees shown in the planner grid
4. Open Project Settings → Project Members tab
5. Drag an employee from position 3 to position 1
6. Click OK to save
7. Return to the planner grid for the same project
8. Verify employee order matches the new DnD order from Project Members
test_cases.expected_result: Planner employee display order matches the order configured in Project Settings → Project Members. Reordering members updates the planner grid order.
test_cases.notes: Project Settings icon: .planner__project-group-add .uikit-button. Members tab allows DnD reordering.

test_cases.title: #3258 — Project sorting alphabetical + green highlight on new task
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Employee assigned to 2+ projects, one having tasks added recently.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Verify projects are listed in alphabetical order in the left sidebar
4. Add a new task to a project via the search/add task input
5. Verify the newly added task row appears with a green highlight/animation
6. Verify project list order remains alphabetical after task addition
test_cases.expected_result: Projects sorted alphabetically. Newly added tasks show a transient green highlight. Alphabetical order is maintained after adding tasks.
test_cases.notes: #3258 was a major UI refactoring commit. Green highlight was part of the new UX.

test_cases.title: #3308 — DnD order persists across date navigation
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with 3+ tasks, editing mode enabled.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab, select project
3. Enter editing mode
4. Reorder tasks via DnD (move task 3 to position 1)
5. Note the new task order
6. Navigate to next day using date navigation arrow
7. Verify task order on the new day matches the reordered sequence
8. Navigate back to original day
9. Verify order is still the reordered sequence
test_cases.expected_result: DnD task order persists when navigating between dates. The reorder is saved to the backend (linked-list position system) and displayed consistently across dates.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3308: order was only stored in frontend state and lost on date change. Fix uses PATCH /v1/assignments/reorder.

test_cases.title: #2832 — Changes visible via WebSocket without manual refresh
test_cases.preconditions: Two browser sessions logged into the same project. PM editing mode enabled in session A.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM in browser session A, enter editing mode on project
2. Login as same or different PM in browser session B, viewing same project
3. In session A: edit hours for an employee on a specific date (e.g., change 4h to 6h)
4. In session B: verify the hours update appears within 2-3 seconds WITHOUT manual refresh
5. In session A: add a new task to an employee
6. In session B: verify the new task row appears in real-time
test_cases.expected_result: WebSocket broadcasts PATCH and ADD events. Session B sees changes from session A in real-time without page refresh.
test_cases.notes: Requires two parallel browser sessions. WebSocket topic: /topic/assignments/{projectId}.

test_cases.title: #3406 — Refresh button error handling
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with tracker configured but in an error state or with connection issues.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select project with tracker
4. Click 'Refresh tickets' from Actions dropdown
5. If tracker is unreachable: verify error banner appears with descriptive message
6. Verify the error banner text includes the tracker name and error type
7. Verify the planner remains functional (not stuck in loading state)
8. Verify clicking Refresh again retries the operation
test_cases.expected_result: Error banner shows when tracker refresh fails. Message is informative (e.g., 'Failed to load from tracker: connection timeout'). Planner remains usable. Retry is possible.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3406: error notification was not shown on refresh failure. Banner selector: [class*='notification'] or getByText with error message.

test_cases.title: #2447 — Open for editing preserves existing assignments
test_cases.preconditions:
PM logged in. Project where an employee already has manually entered hours.
Query: SELECT pa.project_id, pa.employee_id FROM planner_assignment pa WHERE pa.hours > 0 AND pa.date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days' GROUP BY pa.project_id, pa.employee_id LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select project, note existing hours for an employee
4. Click 'Open for editing' on that employee
5. Verify generate API creates only new task slots — does NOT overwrite existing hours
6. Verify the existing manual hours remain unchanged
7. DB-CHECK: SELECT hours FROM planner_assignment WHERE employee_id = <id> AND project_id = <pid> AND date = <date>
test_cases.expected_result: 'Open for editing' generates empty assignment slots for tasks without entries. Existing hours/assignments are NOT modified or zeroed out.
test_cases.notes: The generate endpoint (POST /v1/assignments/generate) should not overwrite existing assignments.

test_cases.title: #2302 — Tracker sync does not zero out TTT-only tasks
test_cases.preconditions:
PM logged in. Project with tracker configured. Employee has hours on a TTT-only task (task not in tracker).
Query: SELECT pa.project_id, pa.employee_id, t.name FROM planner_assignment pa JOIN task t ON pa.task_id = t.id WHERE pa.hours > 0 AND t.tracker_task_id IS NULL AND pa.date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days' LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab, select project with tracker
3. Note employee with hours on a TTT-only task (no tracker link)
4. Note the exact hours value
5. Open Actions → 'Download hours from tracker'
6. Wait for sync to complete
7. Verify TTT-only task hours remain unchanged
8. DB-CHECK: SELECT hours FROM planner_assignment WHERE task_id = <ttt_only_task_id> AND employee_id = <id>
test_cases.expected_result: Tracker sync only updates hours for tracker-linked tasks. TTT-only tasks (tracker_task_id IS NULL) retain their hours.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2302: after tracker sync, hours on TTT-only tasks were being zeroed. This is a data loss scenario.

test_cases.title: #2338 — Tracker timeout shows partial success state
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with tracker. Network conditions that may cause timeout (or large project).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab, select large project with many tracker tasks
3. Open Actions → 'Download hours from tracker'
4. If operation times out: verify error notification appears
5. Verify any tasks that DID sync before timeout show updated hours
6. Verify tasks that did NOT sync retain their previous hours
7. Verify no data corruption (hours are either old or correctly updated, never partially written)
test_cases.expected_result: Timeout during tracker sync shows error notification. Partially synced tasks show correct hours. Non-synced tasks retain original hours. No partial/corrupt data.
test_cases.notes: Difficult to reproduce reliably. May need a large project or simulated slow network.

test_cases.title: Tracker credential types — Jira token vs username/password
test_cases.preconditions:
Two projects: one with Jira Token auth, one with Username/Password auth.
Query: SELECT p.id, p.name, t.type, t.auth_type FROM project p JOIN tracker t ON p.tracker_id = t.id WHERE t.auth_type IN ('TOKEN', 'PASSWORD') LIMIT 2
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select project with Jira Token auth tracker
4. Open Actions → 'Load from tracker'
5. Verify sync succeeds with token-based auth
6. Switch to project with Username/Password auth tracker
7. Open Actions → 'Load from tracker'
8. Verify sync succeeds with password-based auth
9. If either fails: verify error message mentions auth type
test_cases.expected_result: Both credential types (Jira PAT token and username/password) work for tracker sync. Error messages on auth failure are specific to the credential type.
test_cases.notes: Tracker types in TTT: Jira, GitLab, Redmine, YouTrack, Toggl, Clockify, ClickUp. Auth types: TOKEN, PASSWORD.

test_cases.title: Load from tracker vs Refresh tickets — distinct behaviors
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with tracker configured and existing tracked tasks.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab, select project with tracker
3. Open Actions dropdown and note available tracker options
4. Click 'Refresh tickets' — verify it updates the task list (names, new tickets) but NOT hours
5. Note that hours values remain unchanged after 'Refresh tickets'
6. Click 'Download hours from tracker' — verify it updates hours values for the current date range
7. Compare: Refresh = task metadata, Download = hours data
test_cases.expected_result: 'Refresh tickets' syncs task names/IDs from tracker without changing hours. 'Download hours from tracker' syncs actual reported hours. These are distinct operations with different outcomes.
test_cases.notes: Users confuse these two operations. Refresh = GET tasks from tracker. Download = GET hours/worklogs.

test_cases.title: Download hours from Actions dropdown UI flow
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with tracker and at least one employee with tracker hours.
test_cases.steps:

4. Open 'Actions' dropdown
5. Click 'Download hours from tracker'
6. Verify loading indicator appears during sync
7. Verify hours cells update with tracker data after sync completes
8. Verify success notification or banner appears
test_cases.expected_result: Download hours operation shows loading state, then updates hours cells with tracker data. Success feedback is shown.
test_cases.notes: Actions dropdown items: 'Copy table', 'Download hours from tracker', 'Approve all', 'Refresh tickets'.

test_cases.title: #3296 — Approve all not reset by subsequent tracker import

test_cases.title: Tracker not configured — per-employee error state
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Employee without personal tracker credentials on a project that requires them.
test_cases.steps:

2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select project with tracker requiring per-employee credentials
4. Click 'Open for editing' on an employee who has NOT configured tracker
5. Attempt to load hours from tracker for that employee
6. Verify error banner or indicator specific to that employee
7. Verify other employees with configured trackers can still sync normally
test_cases.expected_result: Error is scoped to the specific employee without tracker credentials. Other employees' tracker operations are unaffected. Error message indicates credential issue.
test_cases.notes: Each employee may need their own tracker PAT. Missing credentials should show per-employee error, not block the whole project.

8. Verify the dropdown DOES show that employee
test_cases.expected_result: Member dropdown filters out employees already in the project. Only available employees appear in suggestions.
test_cases.notes: Prevents duplicate member additions. Combobox uses typeahead filtering.

test_cases.title: Project Settings — inline role editing with Enter to save

test_cases.title: Project Settings — tag CRUD with notifications

test_cases.title: Create a close tag — happy path (UI)

Project with tracker integration. User is PM or SPM of the project.
Query: SELECT p.id, p.name FROM ttt_backend.project p JOIN ttt_backend.employee e ON p.manager_id = e.id WHERE e.enabled = true AND p.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the project manager
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects tab
3. Select the project from dropdown
4. Click the Project Settings icon (gear icon at far right of table header)
5. In the Project Settings modal, click 'Tasks closing' tab
6. In the 'Add a tag' input field, type '[closed]'
7. Click the '+' button
8. Verify 'Changes saved' notification appears
9. Verify '[closed]' appears in the tags table
10. Verify the new tag row has green left-border highlight
11. Verify the table auto-scrolled to the new tag
test_cases.expected_result: Tag '[closed]' created and visible in tags table. Green highlight on new row. 'Changes saved' notification displayed. Auto-scroll to new item.
test_cases.notes: POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags returns HTTP 200 (not 201). Immediate save, no confirmation needed.

test_cases.title: Create duplicate tag — idempotent behavior
test_cases.preconditions:
Project with existing tag '[closed]'.
SETUP: Via API — POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags with {"tag":"[closed]"}
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag '[closed]' for the project
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects > select project > Project Settings > Tasks closing
3. Type '[closed]' in the input field (same as existing tag)
4. Click '+' button
5. Verify behavior: either silent no-op or 'Changes saved' notification
6. Verify only ONE '[closed]' tag exists in the table (no duplicate)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags/{tagId}
test_cases.expected_result: Duplicate create is idempotent — returns existing tag. No duplicate row in table. Backend catches DataIntegrityViolationException and returns existing entity.
test_cases.notes: Backend uses REQUIRES_NEW transaction; on unique constraint violation, catches and returns existing tag.

test_cases.title: Create blank/whitespace tag — validation error
test_cases.preconditions: User is PM of a project.

4. Verify validation error or button disabled
5. Type '   ' (spaces only) and click '+'
6. Verify validation error: 'Tag must not be blank'
test_cases.expected_result: Blank and whitespace-only tags are rejected. @NotBlank validation enforced. HTTP 400 with error message.
test_cases.notes: Backend validates @NotBlank on tag field. Frontend may also have client-side validation.

test_cases.title: Permission — employee can list tags but cannot create

Plain employee (not PM/SPM/admin) on a project with tags.

test_cases.title: Apply — assignment without reports gets closed
test_cases.preconditions:
Project with tag matching an assignment's Info column. Assignment has NO reports on selected date.
SETUP: Via API — create tag, ensure assignment with matching ticket_info exists.
Query: SELECT ta.id, t.ticket_info FROM ttt_backend.task_assignment ta JOIN ttt_backend.task t ON ta.task_id = t.id WHERE ta.project_id = {projectId} AND t.ticket_info IS NOT NULL AND t.ticket_info != '' LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag matching an existing assignment's ticket_info
SETUP: Ensure the assignment has no task_report for the selected date
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects > select project
3. Verify the matching assignment is visible in the table
4. Click Project Settings icon > Tasks closing tab
5. Verify the matching tag is listed
6. Click OK button
7. Verify loading spinner appears
8. Verify page reloads automatically
9. After reload: verify the matching assignment is NO LONGER visible in the table
DB-CHECK: SELECT closed FROM ttt_backend.task_assignment WHERE id = {assignmentId} → expect true
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the tag
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment with matching tag and no reports is closed (closed=true). Row disappears from planner table. WebSocket PATCH event published.
test_cases.notes: POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags/apply with {date}. Requires the apply endpoint to be deployed.

test_cases.title: Apply — assignment WITH reports stays visible
test_cases.preconditions:
Project with tag matching an assignment. Assignment HAS reports on selected date.
SETUP: Via API — create tag, ensure assignment has task_report for the date.
Query: SELECT ta.id FROM ttt_backend.task_assignment ta JOIN ttt_backend.task t ON ta.task_id = t.id JOIN ttt_backend.task_report tr ON tr.task = t.id AND tr.executor = ta.employee_id WHERE ta.project_id = {projectId} AND t.ticket_info ILIKE '%{tag}%' AND tr.report_date = '{date}' LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag, verify assignment has reports on date
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects > select project
3. Note the assignment row with matching tag AND reported hours
4. Open Project Settings > Tasks closing > click OK
5. Wait for page reload
6. Verify the assignment is STILL visible in the table (because it has reports)
DB-CHECK: SELECT closed FROM ttt_backend.task_assignment WHERE id = {id} → may be true, but hasReportOnDate prevents disappearance
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment with reports stays visible even when tag matches. hasReportOnDate() returns true → close event NOT published. Hours data preserved.
test_cases.notes: Core safety rule: reported hours are never hidden by close-by-tag.

test_cases.title: Apply — case-insensitive matching
test_cases.preconditions:
Assignment with ticket_info '[CLOSED]' (uppercase). Tag 'closed' (lowercase).

2. Navigate to Planner > Projects
3. Verify an assignment with Info '[CLOSED]' is visible
4. Open Project Settings > Tasks closing > click OK
5. After reload: verify the '[CLOSED]' assignment is closed
DB-CHECK: Verify StringUtils.containsIgnoreCase matches 'closed' in '[CLOSED]'
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the tag
test_cases.expected_result: Case-insensitive match: tag 'closed' matches '[CLOSED]', 'Closed', 'already-closed', etc. Uses StringUtils.containsIgnoreCase().

test_cases.title: Apply — substring matching (tag matches partial text)

2. Trigger apply via OK
3. After reload: verify assignment is closed
Note: This also means tag 'fix' would match 'prefix', 'fixed', 'fixture'
test_cases.expected_result: Substring matching: 'closed' matches any text containing 'closed'. Uses containsIgnoreCase (not exact match).
test_cases.notes: Known false positive risk: short tags can match unintended text.

test_cases.title: Apply — false positive: tag 'Done' matches 'Donetskaya'

test_cases.expected_result: False positive: 'Done' matches 'Donetskaya'. This is acknowledged and accepted by stakeholders. Substring matching has inherent false positive risk.
test_cases.notes: Stakeholders explicitly acknowledged this corner case.

test_cases.title: Apply on specific date — only affects selected date
test_cases.preconditions:
Project with close tag. Assignments on multiple dates.

4. After reload: verify Day1 assignments closed
5. Navigate to Day2
6. Verify Day2 matching assignments are STILL visible (not affected)
DB-CHECK: Check closed status for both days' assignments
test_cases.expected_result: Apply is date-scoped. POST /apply sends {date: currentDay}. Only the selected date's assignments are processed.
test_cases.notes: Frontend sends currentDay from Redux store. Backend uses section.date or section.startDate.

test_cases.title: Apply with no tags — no API call, no reload
test_cases.preconditions: Project with NO close tags configured.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects > select project with no tags
3. Open Project Settings
4. Click OK
5. Verify: page does NOT reload (no API call made)
6. Verify: modal closes normally
DEBUG: Check browser DevTools Network tab — no POST /close-tags/apply request
test_cases.expected_result: Empty-tags guard: if projectTags.length === 0, saga returns immediately. No apply API call. No page reload.
test_cases.notes: Guard added in !5341. Critical for projects without close-by-tag configuration.

test_cases.title: Apply — page reloads after successful apply
test_cases.preconditions: Project with tags.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a tag
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects
3. Open Project Settings > click OK
4. Verify loading spinner appears (startLoadingEmployeeModal)
5. Verify page reloads (window.location.reload)
6. After reload: verify planner state is fresh (assignments updated)
test_cases.expected_result: After apply: loading spinner shown, then window.location.reload() refreshes the page. Full state refresh.
test_cases.notes: window.location.reload() is a blunt approach. The finally block (stopLoading + closeModal) is dead code since page reloads.

test_cases.title: Apply from Project Members tab — triggers if tags exist
test_cases.preconditions: Project with close tags. User is on Project Members tab.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a tag
