2. Navigate to Project Settings modal
3. Stay on 'Project members' tab (do NOT switch to Tasks closing)
4. Click OK
5. Verify: apply is STILL triggered (if tags exist)
6. Verify: page reloads
Note: Both tabs share the same OK handler after !5341
test_cases.expected_result: Both tabs' OK button triggers apply. If project has tags, apply runs regardless of which tab is active. Mitigated by empty-tags guard.
test_cases.notes: Design side effect: user who only changed Project Members still triggers close-tags apply.

test_cases.title: Apply — generated (not-yet-opened) assignments also closed
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee whose assignments are NOT yet 'opened for editing' (readOnly). Project has matching tag.

4. Trigger apply via OK
5. After reload: verify the generated assignments for that employee are also processed
DB-CHECK: SELECT id, closed FROM ttt_backend.task_assignment WHERE employee_id = {id} AND closed = true → verify new closed records created
test_cases.expected_result: Close-by-tag handles generated (id=null) assignments by creating them as closed via createForCloseByTag(). Works even for employees not yet 'opened for editing'.
test_cases.notes: Silent failure: if createForCloseByTag throws, error is swallowed with log.debug() only.

test_cases.title: Apply after 'Open for editing' — newly generated assignments eligible

test_cases.expected_result: Sequence: Open for editing → Apply. Newly generated assignments with matching Info are eligible for closing.

test_cases.title: Apply — multiple tags, partial matches

Project with 3 tags. Assignments: some match tag1, some match tag2, some match none.

3. Trigger apply
4. After reload: verify 3 assignments closed (matching ones without reports)
5. Verify 2 non-matching assignments still visible
test_cases.expected_result: Each assignment checked against ALL tags. An assignment matching ANY tag is eligible for closing. Non-matching assignments unaffected.

test_cases.title: Apply — assignment with blank ticket_info is skipped

2. Verify the blank-info assignment is NOT closed
DB-CHECK: SELECT t.ticket_info, ta.closed FROM ttt_backend.task_assignment ta JOIN ttt_backend.task t ON ta.task_id = t.id WHERE ta.project_id = {id} AND (t.ticket_info IS NULL OR t.ticket_info = '')
test_cases.expected_result: Assignments with null/blank ticket_info are skipped. No false positives on empty data.
test_cases.notes: Code: if ticketInfo is blank, skip.

test_cases.title: Apply endpoint — API direct call

test_cases.title: Bug 1 regression — popup closes only via OK button

test_cases.title: Bug 3 regression — correct column header in Tasks Closing tab

test_cases.title: Bug 6 — cannot reopen popup on heavy data project
test_cases.preconditions:
Heavy data project (many assignments, e.g., DirectEnergie-ODC-like). Tags previously configured.
Query: SELECT p.id, p.name, COUNT(ta.id) FROM ttt_backend.project p JOIN ttt_backend.task_assignment ta ON ta.project_id = p.id GROUP BY p.id, p.name ORDER BY COUNT(ta.id) DESC LIMIT 5

4. After reload: attempt to reopen Project Settings

test_cases.title: Apply error handling — silent failure on backend error

Query: SELECT e.login, edr.last_approved_date FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_dayoff_request edr ON edr.employee = e.id WHERE edr.last_approved_date >= (SELECT start_date FROM ttt_backend.office_period WHERE type = 'APPROVE' LIMIT 1) AND edr.last_approved_date < CURRENT_DATE AND edr.status NOT IN ('DELETED', 'DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR') AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY edr.last_approved_date DESC LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:

test_cases.title: Previous year day-offs — all edit icons hidden

test_cases.title: Future holiday: minDate still uses original date (ST-4)

test_cases.expected_result: Multiple validation errors returned in errors[] array. Each with field name, code, and message.

3. Note which field contains the specific crossing error code
4. Via API — PUT /vacations/{id} with overlapping dates (update endpoint)
5. Compare error format between create and update
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE

test_cases.title: Batch deadlock on concurrent operations
test_cases.preconditions: Ability to send concurrent API requests.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — simultaneously send 3 vacation create requests for the same employee
2. Observe responses — at least 1 should succeed
3. Check for 500 errors with 'deadlock detected' message
4. Verify VacationRecalculationService row contention triggers PostgreSQL deadlock
test_cases.expected_result: Concurrent vacation operations may cause PostgreSQL deadlocks. CannotAcquireLockException thrown. Not all requests succeed.
test_cases.notes: Root: employee_vacation table row contention during FIFO redistribution. Mitigation: 2+ second gaps.
